title: Good UV protection
text: I drive a truck for 6-11 hours a day with my left arm in the sun much of that time. These aren't made from very sturdy material, but they keep my arm's color even, which means they're blocking the sun. They're comfortable and cool in summer heat (up to 110º). Other UV block shirts are usually much more expensive, and I haven't found them to be much sturdier. These are cheap enough to throw away when I snag one on something, and I've become more subconciously aware of things they can snag on, so they last much longer now.
